title: The Legends of Rocks, Roger, and Oden Surface
description: On pages 3–6, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Whitebeard Pirates, Unknown person #4, Whitebeard as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The new world marine HQ

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I suppose you're quite pleased, Issho.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You and your lot have longed for the abolition of the Shichibuki system for some time.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I, myself, was against it, but... the will of the kings cannot be overturned.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and the "sacrifices" necessary for the continued existence of the Shichibukai?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You've never been on the chopping block yourself! You have no idea what it's like.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All that's left is to put our faith in SSG.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The power balances of this world shall surely...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: SHift greatly!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Aye, in directions we cannot predict!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I hear tell that in Wano, big mom and Kaido

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: intend to join forces.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: What the-?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Rocks' second coming?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't speak of it like some sort of Fable.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: This is real.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: When the rains of disaster come,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: They pour!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: The yonko have joined hands?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: of all things?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Now?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: to be precise, it seems they "plan" to join forces.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They have yet to cause any real damage.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But we do not know what they intend to achieve, nor can we anticipate how best to counter them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And as for the legendary rocks pirates, information remains quite scamt...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hmmm... big mom and Kaido have been, for many years, as cat and dog.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: This is certainly unforeseen.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quite some time ago, the rocks pirates formed when a band of pirates

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: The following information

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Gathered on the pirate island Beehive for a particular get-rich-quick scheme. A unique bunch.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They would constantly kill each other off. Even at sea, on the same ship. A violent crew.

dialogue.speaker: Whitebeard Pirates
dialogue.text: With their captain, rocks, at the lead,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Their members included Whitebeard, Big Mom, and Kaido, all in their younger years.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: WhAA AAA??!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yes. Once upon a time, all three of them flew the same flag.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: TH- THAT'S UNBELIEV- ABLE!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There are others from the rocks pirates who later made names for themselves.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Golden Lion. Silver axe. Captain John, Wang Zhi.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You've gotta be kidding! How do all of those people hail from the same dark corner of history?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, one reason is that all of these pirates got along so badly that very few around them lived long enough to tell the tale.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: their captain, rocks, aimed to become pirate king.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And he bared the fangs of his crew at the world government, almost like a terrorist organization.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 42 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
